---
title: "Project Independence"
author: "Theo Schouten"
date: "2026-03-06"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r, echo=FALSE}
# ============================================================================
# Lagged Logistic Regression Test for Independence Between Trials
# Tests whether the previous trial's outcome predicts the current trial
# If the lag term is significant, trials are not independent
# ============================================================================

library(tidyverse)

# --- 1. Load and Clean Data ---------------------------------------------------

data <- read.csv("Current training csv.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

data <- data %>%
  select(-considerations) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Success))

data$Success <- as.integer(data$Success)
data$Trick   <- as.factor(data$Trick)

# Remove the single erroneous "fast accel " entry
data <- data %>% filter(Trick != "fast accel ")

# --- 2. Create Lagged Variable Within Each Trick -----------------------------
# For each trick, the lag is the previous trial's outcome.
# The first trial of each trick has no lag and is dropped.
#
# Logic: If the previous outcome predicts the current outcome,
# then the trials are not independent.

data <- data %>%
  group_by(Trick) %>%
  mutate(Lag_Success = lag(Success, n = 1)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Drop rows with no lag (first trial per trick)
data_lagged <- data %>% filter(!is.na(Lag_Success))

data_lagged$Lag_Success <- as.factor(data_lagged$Lag_Success)

cat("Observations with valid lag:", nrow(data_lagged), "\n\n")

# --- 3. Overall Lagged Model --------------------------------------------------
# Tests whether the previous trial's outcome predicts the current one
# across all tricks pooled together.

cat("=== Overall Lagged Logistic Regression ===\n")
cat("H0: Previous outcome does not predict current outcome (independent trials)\n")
cat("H1: Previous outcome predicts current outcome (dependent trials)\n\n")

model_no_lag  <- glm(Success ~ Trick,
                     data = data_lagged, family = binomial(link = "logit"))

model_with_lag <- glm(Success ~ Trick + Lag_Success,
                      data = data_lagged, family = binomial(link = "logit"))

# Likelihood ratio test: does adding the lag improve the model?
lr_test <- anova(model_no_lag, model_with_lag, test = "Chisq")
cat("Likelihood Ratio Test — Adding Lag Term:\n")
print(lr_test)

cat("\n--- Lag Coefficient from Full Model ---\n")
lag_coef <- summary(model_with_lag)$coefficients["Lag_Success1", ]
cat(sprintf("Lag Estimate:  %0.4f\n", lag_coef["Estimate"]))
cat(sprintf("Std. Error:    %0.4f\n", lag_coef["Std. Error"]))
cat(sprintf("z value:       %0.4f\n", lag_coef["z value"]))
cat(sprintf("p-value:       %0.4f\n", lag_coef["Pr(>|z|)"]))

if (lag_coef["Pr(>|z|)"] < 0.05) {
  cat("\nConclusion: The lag term IS significant — previous trial outcome\n")
  cat("predicts the current one. Trials are NOT independent overall.\n")
} else {
  cat("\nConclusion: The lag term is NOT significant — no evidence that the\n")
  cat("previous trial outcome predicts the current one. Trials appear independent.\n")
}

# --- 4. Trick-by-Trick Lagged Models -----------------------------------------
# Fit a separate lagged logistic regression for each trick to see
# if dependence exists within specific tricks.

cat("\n\n=== Trick-by-Trick Lagged Logistic Regression ===\n")
cat("For each trick: Success ~ Lag_Success (logit link)\n\n")

tricks <- levels(droplevels(data_lagged$Trick))

results <- data.frame(
  Trick        = character(),
  n            = integer(),
  Lag_Estimate = numeric(),
  Std_Error    = numeric(),
  z_value      = numeric(),
  p_value      = numeric(),
  conclusion   = character(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

for (trick in tricks) {
  
  subset <- data_lagged %>% filter(Trick == trick)
  n      <- nrow(subset)
  
  # Need enough data and variation in both Success and Lag
  if (n < 10 ||
      length(unique(subset$Success)) < 2 ||
      length(unique(subset$Lag_Success)) < 2) {
    cat(sprintf("%-20s  n = %3d  — Skipped (insufficient data or no variation)\n",
                trick, n))
    next
  }
  
  model <- glm(Success ~ Lag_Success,
               data = subset, family = binomial(link = "logit"))
  
  coefs <- summary(model)$coefficients
  
  if ("Lag_Success1" %in% rownames(coefs)) {
    lag_row    <- coefs["Lag_Success1", ]
    conclusion <- ifelse(lag_row["Pr(>|z|)"] < 0.05,
                         "DEPENDENT (reject H0)",
                         "Independent (fail to reject H0)")
    
    results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
      Trick        = trick,
      n            = n,
      Lag_Estimate = round(lag_row["Estimate"], 4),
      Std_Error    = round(lag_row["Std. Error"], 4),
      z_value      = round(lag_row["z value"], 4),
      p_value      = round(lag_row["Pr(>|z|)"], 4),
      conclusion   = conclusion
    ))
  }
}

print(results, right = FALSE)


cat("\n=== Summary ===\n")
n_tested      <- nrow(results)
n_dependent   <- sum(results$p_value < 0.05)
n_independent <- n_tested - n_dependent

cat(sprintf("Tricks tested: %d\n", n_tested))
cat(sprintf("Independent (p >= 0.05): %d\n", n_independent))
cat(sprintf("Dependent (p < 0.05):    %d\n", n_dependent))

if (n_dependent == 0) {
  cat("\nConclusion: No evidence that previous trial outcomes predict current\n")
  cat("outcomes for any trick. Trials appear independent.\n")
} else {
  cat("\nTricks showing dependence (previous outcome predicts current):\n")
  print(results %>% filter(p_value < 0.05) %>% select(Trick, Lag_Estimate, p_value))
}

```
